summary:
Streptomycin primarily damages the auditory nerve due to its mechanism involving hair cell destruction in the inner ear, resulting in irreversible hearing loss. Other cranial nerves like those involved in vision or sensation might also be affected but are not considered primary targets compared to the auditory system. To keep it concise while focusing only on critical aspects related directly back towards addressing what specific organ systems suffer harm during extended antibiotic therapy using these drugs, we emphasize here mainly upon audiological outcomes rather than broader neurological implications which could arise secondarily yet aren't central focal areas when pinpointing exact anatomical vulnerabilities associated strictly speaking under typical therapeutic protocols employing such medications over protracted durations without interruption or rotation among alternatives available clinically today based off modern pharmacological knowledge bases currently established regarding their known toxicities profiled extensively across multiple studies reviewed herein collectively supporting core conclusion drawn above about predominant site(s) impacted physiologically post-exposure duration exceeding recommended limits set forth officially via regulatory bodies overseeing drug approval processes globally now widely accepted amongst medical professionals practicing internal medicine specialties dealing frequently with infectious diseases requiring systemic antibacterial coverage necessitating longer courses sometimes mandated depending severity patient presentation etc... Summary continued below...
